Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap

plot_weight_heatmap.Rd

Renders each weight matrix in an ANN-format brain (a list with a layers element, each layer having $W and $b) as a diverging blue-white-red tile heatmap. Blue = strong inhibitory connections, red = strong excitatory connections, white = near-zero weights. One panel per layer.

Agents under strong selection often show structured weight matrices (certain input-to-hidden connections consistently strong) compared to the near-random patterns of newly initialised founders.

Usage

plot_weight_heatmap(ann, title = "Neural genome")

Arguments

ann

A brain list with a $layers element, as returned by env$agents[[i]]$brain for ANN brain types. Must be an R list (not a JuliaConnectoR proxy).

title

Character scalar prepended to each panel title. Default: "Neural genome".

Value

A patchwork::wrap_plots() object with one panel per layer.

Details

Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap

See also

visualize_progress()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
env <- run_clade(default_specs())
# Access brain data (requires converting from Julia proxy first)
# plot_weight_heatmap(brain_list)
} # }
